role_overview: Would you like to shape the future of autonomous urban mobility together with us?Turtle Mobility is seeking a Verification Engineer to ensure system and software correctness through structured testing in simulated and lab environments. This role is critical to validating vehicle functionality before it reaches real roads and real users.
The position is well suited for engineers who enjoy finding issues early, working close to the system, and validating real vehicle behavior — not just specifications.
responsibilities: Perform MIL (ModelintheLoop), SIL (SoftwareintheLoop), and HIL (HardwareintheLoop) testing for vehicle systems and features
Develop, review, and execute test cases, test scenarios, and test plans based on system and software requirements
Create and maintain test scripts and automation using CAPL or similar scripting languages
Configure and operate test benches, ECUs, and simulation environments
Validate functional behavior of vehicle systems, including drivebywire, joystick controls, braking, powertrain, and body electronics
Analyze test results, logs, and traces to identify defects and functional gaps
Perform rootcause analysis and work closely with development teams to support issue resolution
Ensure requirement traceability between specifications, test cases, and test results
Document verification results and support release readiness decisions
Contribute to improving verification processes, coverage, and efficiency
requirements: Strong experience with CANoe, CANalyzer, VT Studio, or similar tools
Knowledge of CAPL scripting for test automation
Understanding of automotive EE systems and communication protocols
Experience working with simulation and lab environments
Ability to work independently and methodically in testing workflows
experience: Preferred 2–5 years of experience in automotive verification, validation, or testing
Handson experience is valued over formal qualifications
Experience gained through:
OEMs, Tier1 suppliers, or automotive startups
SAE BAJA, Formula SAE, Formula Student, SUPRA, or similar competitions
University, student, or independent verification and testing projects
…is considered equivalent to traditional professional experience.

description: Turtle Mobility is building the world’s first joystickbased electric microcar designed from the ground up for modern urban mobility. We strip away unnecessary complexity, focus on minimalistic and humancentric design, and combine autonomouslike convenience with real human control through remote driving.We build real vehicles for real cities.
